nodes.context: Large Russian gang based in Saint Petersburg, involved in racketeering, drug trafficking, money laundering.
Another leading criminal group in Leningrad, rival of Tambovskaya Bratva.
Criminal group from Tatarstan, fought Tambovskaya Bratva for control of energy trade.
Criminal organization from Chechnya, introduced Russian mafia to Japanese yakuza.
Japanese organized crime group from Kobe-Osaka, connected via Kinichi Kamiyasu.
Criminal group suspected of attempting to kill Kumarin.
Russian mafia group, rival of Tambovskaya Bratva according to infobox.
Criminal organization listed as ally of Tambovskaya Bratva in infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1989, the gang clashed with Malyshev's Gang, another leading criminal group of Leningrad, in a bloody armed conflict.
edges.context: Tambovskaya Bratva fought Malyshevskaya OGG in a violent conflict in 1989.

edges.evidence_quote: He later briefly allied with Alexander Malyshev, who had controlled the narcotics trade since 1993 after ousting the Azerbaijani mafia from the trade, to fight the Kazan gang from Tatarstan.
edges.context: Tambovskaya Bratva briefly allied with Malyshevskaya OGG to fight the Kazan gang.

edges.evidence_quote: During the later half of 1993 and into 1994 and with bank accounts in New York, Vladimir I. Dyakov of the Kazan Gang, which had many very devout Muslim Tatars, fought the Tambov Gang for control of St. Petersburg's energy trade.
edges.context: Tambovskaya Bratva fought the Kazan Gang for control of St. Petersburg's energy trade in 1993-1994.

edges.evidence_quote: He believed that the Velikie Luki were responsible and, later, Viktor Gavrilenkov and Nikolai Gavrilenkov were shot with Viktor Gavrilenkov being killed in the bedlam.
edges.context: Tambovskaya Bratva believed Velikie Luki were responsible for an assassination attempt on Kumarin, leading to retaliation.

edges.evidence_quote: Temo Davidov, a member of the Chechen mafia which had caused Manvel to flee the Soviet Union for Stockholm where Kinichi Kamiyasu gave him a job and a Polish passport, introduced the Russian mafia, Petrov, Kuzmin, and Malyshev, to the son of a Japanese yakuza from Kobe-Osaka, Kinichi Kamiyasu.
edges.context: A member of the Chechen mafia introduced Tambovskaya Bratva associates to Japanese yakuza.

edges.evidence_quote: Temo Davidov, a member of the Chechen mafia ... introduced the Russian mafia, Petrov, Kuzmin, and Malyshev, to the son of a Japanese yakuza from Kobe-Osaka, Kinichi Kamiyasu.
edges.context: Tambovskaya Bratva associates were introduced to Japanese yakuza, indicating a cooperative connection.
Infobox lists Solntsevskaya Bratva as a rival of Tambovskaya Bratva.
Infobox lists Obshchak as an ally of Tambovskaya Bratva.
